verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46/TGN51) is a type I integral membrane protein that resides in the trans-Golgi network and cycles between the TGN and early endosomes. Its membrane anchor is embedded in the TGN/endosomal membranes, placing the bulk of the protein in the endomembrane system lumen — not on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane. It has no documented stable plasma membrane surface pool, no CD designation, no surface-directed therapeutics, and no evidence of GPI anchor or outer-leaflet mechanism. Cycling through TGN/endosomes does not constitute plasma membrane surface accessibility.
